selected_important_turn.text: I as well would need some extra food and water. I have a large family and they eat a ton. 3 kids...
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is an early escalation: the opponent had just disclosed needs (prosocial info) and you immediately signaled competing claims for the same scarce goods. That shift turned an integrative opportunity into a more zero-sum framing and materially raised the stakes for subsequent offers.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It is unnecessarily competitive and misses a clear chance to build on the opponent's disclosure to find tradeoffs. By emphasizing your own competing need without proposing trades or eliciting the opponent's flexibility, you made agreement harder and signaled lower willingness to cooperate.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: A more constructive move would acknowledge their needs, signal empathy, and immediately seek tradeable differences (e.g., ask about their surplus in firewood or priorities between water vs food) or propose explicit exchanges rather than simply claiming the same items. Use justification sparingly and pair it with a concrete integrative offer or question about flexibility.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by opening integrative trade possibilities instead of locking into competition)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (more balanced exchange if tradeoffs are explored)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (less escalation and more cooperative tone will speed agreement)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (empathy + willingness to trade builds trust)

dialogue.text: I am good. Do you have any preferences on which items you would like most?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: yes i really need a high amount of water and food.i have firewood in excess
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I as well would need some extra food and water. I have a large family and they eat a ton. 3 kids...
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 water and 1 food. Firewood is low priority for me. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand you need more water and food. Since I value firewood highly, maybe we can trade some firewood for extra food or water? What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m willing to trade 1 firewood for 1 food. That way, I get more food without sacrificing too much. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm okay with trading 1 firewood for 1 food. Let's see if we can work out a deal that covers all the packages.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'll take 2 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood. That gives me 10 points. You can have the remaining 1 water, 1 food, and 2 firewood. Let me know if you agree.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I see. Let me check the math. If I take 2 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood, that’s 10 points. You’re getting 1 water, 1 food, and 2 firewood. That’s 11 points. It’s a fair split. Do you agree?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to the split. You get 2 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood. I get 1 water, 1 food, and 2 firewood. It’s a fair deal.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
